message:
refactor(writing): clean up Bakery article and improve Three.js resource disposal

Bakery Article (bakery-article.tsx):
- Remove unused `Users` import from lucide-react
- Remove unused `TieBreakerViz` dynamic import that was no longer referenced
- Remove four self-explanatory comments that added no value:
  - "Real content-based reading time calculation"
  - "Section reveal on scroll"
  - "Scroll Progress"
  - "Footer spacing"

Bakery Visualizations (bakery-visualizations.tsx):
- Refactor BakeryHero Three.js cleanup from ad-hoc pattern to systematic
  disposables array approach:
  - Replace separate `ticketGeos` and `ticketMats` arrays with unified
    `disposables` array that collects all GPU resources needing cleanup
  - Remove custom `scene._cleanup` method that was fragile and non-standard
  - Push geometries and materials to disposables array at creation time
  - Iterate disposables in useEffect cleanup for guaranteed disposal
- Add defensive null checks:
  - `renderer` typed as `THREE.WebGLRenderer | null` with optional chaining
  - `handleResize` typed as `(() => void) | null`
  - Container existence check before removing renderer DOM element

This cleanup reduces code complexity while improving reliability of GPU
resource cleanup, preventing potential WebGL context leaks on unmount.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
